---
title: "Phase 1: Data Foundation"
subtitle: "Kikoff Marketing Mix Model — EDA Summary"
date: 2026-04-28
author: "Data Science Team"
format:
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: false
    preview-links: auto
    theme: default
    transition: none
    width: 1200
    height: 800
    center: false
    hash: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Overview

**Purpose:** Confirm the data foundation going into Phase 2 model build, and document what changed in the updated files received 2026-04-27.

**Audience:** Kikoff team

## Agenda

1. Key numbers at a glance
2. Spend patterns
3. Conversions & LTV
4. Spend-conversion relationship and seasonality
5. Incrementality test results
6. **What changed in the updated data files** ← new
7. Data quality — open items only
8. Immediate next steps

---

# Key Numbers at a Glance

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Modeling window | Q3 2024 – March 2026 |
| Total spend (full history) | $112.9M |
| Daily average spend | $132.5K |
| Mean daily conversions | 2,560 |
| Peak daily conversions | 4,354 (2025-05-30) |
| Top 3 channels | Meta (37%), TikTok (21%), Google (16%) |
| Same-day spend–conversion correlation | r = 0.445 |
| Best carryover lag | 1 day (r = 0.428) |
| Tax season lift | ~2.4× rest-of-year baseline |
| Channels with incrementality priors | 3 (Meta, TikTok, CTV) |

---

# Spend Patterns

## Total Daily Spend Over Time

![](../outputs/P1_02_eda/figures/total_daily_spend.png)

## Channel Composition Over Time

![](../outputs/P1_02_eda/figures/channel_composition_monthly.png)

::: {.notes}
Dashed vertical line marks start of modeling window (Q3 2024). Others (dark red) was flagged in the original data at 9.2% of spend; resolved to ~0.0% in the updated file received 2026-04-27.
:::

## Top Channels by Total Spend

![](../outputs/P1_02_eda/figures/top_10_channels_spend.png)

---

# Conversions & LTV

## Daily Conversions Over Time

![](../outputs/P1_02_eda/figures/daily_conversions.png)

Red dots = statistical outliers (>3σ) — genuine peak-demand days, not errors.

## Daily Cohort LTV Over Time

![](../outputs/P1_02_eda/figures/ltv_per_conversion.png)

`LTV_1YEAR` is the **total 1-year revenue of all customers acquired that day** — an aggregate. Mean ≈ $293.7K/day at ~2,560 conversions/day implies ~$115 per acquired customer in the updated file.

## Modeling Window: Conversions + Revenue

![](../outputs/P1_02_eda/figures/conversions_and_revenue.png)

---

# Spend-Conversion Relationship & Seasonality

## Same-Day Scatter (Modeling Window Q3 2024 – Mar 2026)

![](../outputs/P1_02_eda/figures/spend_vs_conversions_scatter.png)

## Lag Analysis

![](../outputs/P1_02_eda/figures/spend_conversion_lag.png)

**Reading the chart:** Same-day correlation (r = 0.445) is strongest but partly driven by shared seasonality. Best carryover lag = 1 day (r = 0.428), with a secondary cluster at 5–7 days — this is the adstock signal. Adstock values are model outputs, not inputs.

## Month-of-Year Seasonality

![](../outputs/P1_02_eda/figures/monthly_seasonality.png)

Tax season (Jan–Apr) averages ~2.4× above rest-of-year baseline. Modeled explicitly so channel effects aren't confounded by calendar demand.

---

# Incrementality Tests

## Tests Available — 3 Usable Priors

| Channel | Period | Type | Use in Model |
|---------|--------|------|--------------|
| TikTok | Aug 22 – Sep 15, 2025 | Holdout | Clean — used as prior |
| Meta | May 6 – May 14, 2025 | Holdout (ended early) | Usable — used as prior |
| Meta | Jan 3 – Jan 8, 2026 | Holdout (cancelled) | Wider CI prior |
| CTV | Oct 6 – Nov 2, 2025 | Geo holdout | Clean — used as prior |

All priors are **time-limited** — active only within test window dates, not across full model history.

## iCAC by Channel + Platform

| Channel | Platform | iCAC ($) |
|---------|----------|----------|
| TikTok | iOS | 108.83 |
| TikTok | Android | 81.68 |
| TikTok | Web | 112.12 |
| Meta (May 2025) | iOS | 135.48 |
| Meta (May 2025) | Android | 63.06 |
| Meta (May 2025) | Web | 156.89 |
| CTV | All | 135.00 |

iCAC = incremental customer acquisition cost from each holdout. These anchor each tested channel's prior distribution.

---

# What Changed in the Updated Data

*Files received 2026-04-27 to replace original 2026-04-07 files*

## Spend File: Row-Level Changes

| Period | Original | Updated | Delta | What happened |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2023 | 0 | 320 | **+320** | Full 2023 now included |
| 2024 | 1,448 | 1,429 | −19 | Minor cleanup |
| 2025 | 7,940 | 7,171 | **−769** | Data quality cleanup |
| 2026 | 2,118 | 2,266 | +148 | New coverage through Apr 27 |
| **Total** | **11,506** | **11,186** | **−320** | |

**Net effect on modeling window (Q3 2024+):** −640 rows. All 15 channels still present — no channel was dropped entirely.

## Others / Idle — Effectively Resolved in New File

The Others issue flagged in the original data is **resolved by the data engineering update**:

| | Original file | Updated file |
|---|---|---|
| Others spend (Q3 2024+) | $11,956,257 | $33,863 |
| Others % of window spend | **9.2%** (peaked at 16.3%) | **~0.0%** |

942 Others combos were removed; remaining $33.9K is negligible. The cleaned rows were either reclassified into proper channels or removed as erroneous entries. Others is no longer a modeling concern.

**Other top changes in the modeling window** (1,035 combos removed, 395 added):

| Channel | Combos removed | Combos added |
|---|---|---|
| facebook | 42 | 60 |
| appvertiser | 28 | 0 |
| liftoff | 15 | 40 |
| applovin | 0 | 60 |
| tiktok | 0 | 60 |
| google | 0 | 49 |

## Data Note: 2023-06 and 2023-07 Missing

June and July 2023 have **no rows in either spend file** — this is a genuine data gap, not a chart error. The channel composition chart correctly shows no bars for those two months. Pre-2024 data is outside the modeling window and does not affect the model.

## LTV File: Value and Coverage Changes

| | Original (2026-04-07) | Updated (2026-04-27) |
|---|---|---|
| Row count | 1,193 | 1,213 (+20) |
| Coverage | 2023-01-01 – 2026-04-07 | 2023-01-01 – 2026-04-27 |
| Mean LTV_1YEAR (2024+) | $185,515 | $293,686 |
| **Uplift factor** | — | **1.58× higher** |
| Null LTV dates | 2 (2024-03-09, 2024-03-10) | **0** |

The 1.58× LTV uplift is expected — Abheek confirmed updated values would be "more inflated." The 2 previously null dates are now populated. LTV anomaly windows (2024-02-29–03-11, 2025-10-21–24) still require imputation — method confirmed.

---

# Data Quality — Open Items

Resolved in the new data file (no longer blocking):

- ~~Others / Idle anomalous share~~ — **Resolved.** Others reduced from 9.2% → ~0.0% of modeling window spend

Remaining open items:

| # | Issue | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Time-limited prior mechanism** | Requirement confirmed; implementation approach being finalized |
| 2 | **LTV anomaly imputation** | Two windows (2024-02-29–03-11, 2025-10-21–24) — method confirmed, not yet applied |
| 3 | **Platform normalization** | Transformations defined — applied in preprocessing |

---

# Immediate Next Steps

1. Apply platform normalization + LTV imputation in preprocessing
2. Stand up PyMC-Marketing model on Q3 2024+ slice with corrected data
3. Finalize time-limited prior implementation mechanism
4. First POC: ICAC + iROAS curves for Meta — Web

---

*Phase 1 Data Foundation | Kikoff Marketing Mix Modeling | 2026-04-28*
